stem: For \( f(x)=x^{3}+kx \), find the value of \( k \) that makes \( x=[[x0]] \) a critical point.
For \( f(x) = x + \dfrac{k}{x} \), find the value of \( k \) that makes \( x=[[x0]] \) a critical point.
distractor_rationales: This has the wrong sign for \( k \).
This reports \( x_0 \) itself instead of \( x_0^{2} \).
This has the wrong sign and forgets to square.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: For \( f(x) = x^{3} - 3([[a]])^{2}x + k \), the location of \( f \)'s local minimum (at \( x=[[a]] \)) does not depend on \( k \), but its VALUE does. Find the value of \( k \) that makes that local minimum value equal \( [[V]] \).
distractor_rationales: This has the wrong sign on the shift term.
This uses \( a^{3} \) instead of \( 2a^{3} \) for the shift.
This reports only the shift term, dropping \( V \).
This subtracts \( a^3 \) instead of adding \( 2a^3 \).
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: For \( f(x) = x^{3} + ([[p]])x^{2} + kx \), find the value of \( k \) such that BOTH \( x=[[r1]] \) and \( x=[[r2]] \) are critical points of \( f \).
distractor_rationales: This uses the sum of the roots (times 3) instead of the product.
This forgets the factor of 3 from the leading coefficient.
This uses \( 3(r_1+r_2) \), confusing the sum-of-roots relation with the product.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
